spider-man.name: Spider-Man
spider-man.description: Peter Parker is a superhero who gains spider-like abilities after being bitten by a radioactive spider. Created by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko, he debuted in 1962 and became one of Marvel Comics' most popular characters.

captain-america.name: Captain America
captain-america.description: Steve Rogers is a super-soldier who has fought for freedom and justice since World War II. Created by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1941 and became one of Marvel's greatest hero icons.

daredevil.name: Daredevil
daredevil.description: Matt Murdock is a blind lawyer who, after an accident with radioactive material, had his remaining senses heightened to superhuman levels. Created by Stan Lee and Bill Everett, he debuted in 1964.

hawkeye.name: Hawkeye
hawkeye.description: Clint Barton is an archer and master marksman with no superhuman powers, a longtime member of the Avengers. Created by Stan Lee and Don Heck, he debuted in 1964.

hulk.name: Hulk
hulk.description: Bruce Banner is a scientist who transforms into a green creature of immense strength when enraged, after a gamma radiation accident. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1962.

punisher.name: Punisher
punisher.description: Frank Castle is a former Marine who becomes a ruthless vigilante after his family is murdered, fighting crime with lethal methods. Created by Gerry Conway, John Romita Sr., and Ross Andru, he debuted in 1974.

iron-fist.name: Iron Fist
iron-fist.description: Danny Rand is a martial arts master who obtained the mystical power of the Iron Fist in the city of K'un-Lun. Created by Roy Thomas and Gil Kane, he debuted in 1974.

thor.name: Thor
thor.description: The Asgardian god of thunder and son of Odin, wielder of the enchanted hammer Mjolnir. Created by Stan Lee, Larry Lieber, and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1962.

wolverine.name: Wolverine
wolverine.description: Logan is a mutant with an accelerated healing factor, retractable claws (later coated in adamantium), and heightened senses. Created by Len Wein, John Romita Sr., and Herb Trimpe, he debuted in 1974.

iron-man.name: Iron Man
iron-man.description: Tony Stark is a billionaire engineer who built a technological suit of armor to survive and fight crime. Created by Stan Lee, Larry Lieber, Don Heck, and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1963.

green-arrow.name: Green Arrow
green-arrow.description: Oliver Queen is a millionaire who becomes a vigilante archer after being stranded on a deserted island. Created by Mort Weisinger and George Papp for DC Comics, he debuted in 1941.

invincible.name: Invincible
invincible.description: Mark Grayson is a teenager who discovers he has the same powers as his father, the hero Omni-Man, and takes up the mantle of Invincible. Created by Robert Kirkman and Cory Walker, he debuted in 2003 through Image Comics.

spawn.name: Spawn
spawn.description: Al Simmons is a murdered mercenary who returns from Hell as a Hellspawn, seeking revenge and redemption. Created by Todd McFarlane, he debuted in 1992 through Image Comics.

tex.name: Tex Willer
tex.description: Tex Willer is a former outlaw who becomes a Texas Ranger and defender of justice in the American Old West, as well as an honorary Navajo chief. Created by Gian Luigi Bonelli and Aurelio Galleppini, he debuted in 1948 in Italy.

batman.name: Batman
batman.description: Bruce Wayne is a billionaire who witnessed his parents' murder as a child and vowed to fight crime in Gotham City using no superpowers, only intellect, training, and technology. Created by Bob Kane and Bill Finger, he debuted in 1939.

green-lantern.name: Green Lantern
green-lantern.description: Hal Jordan is a test pilot who receives a power ring from a dying alien, becoming a member of the Green Lantern Corps, an intergalactic police force. Created by John Broome and Gil Kane, he debuted in 1959.

superman.name: Superman
superman.description: Kal-El is an alien from Krypton raised on Earth as Clark Kent, gifted with superhuman strength, speed, and flight thanks to Earth's yellow sun. Created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster, he debuted in 1938 and is considered the first superhero in history.

wonder-woman.name: Wonder Woman
wonder-woman.description: Diana Prince is an Amazon princess from Themyscira with superhuman strength, speed, and combat skills. Created by William Moulton Marston and H. G. Peter, she debuted in 1941.

flash.name: Flash
flash.description: Barry Allen is a forensic scientist who gains superspeed after being struck by lightning and chemicals. Created by Robert Kanigher and Carmine Infantino, he debuted in 1956.

aquaman.name: Aquaman
aquaman.description: Arthur Curry is the King of Atlantis, with superhuman strength, aquatic endurance, and the ability to communicate with sea life. Created by Mort Weisinger and Paul Norris, he debuted in 1941.

cyborg.name: Cyborg
cyborg.description: Victor Stone is an athlete who becomes half human, half machine after an accident, gaining superhuman strength and the ability to interface with any electronic system. Created by Marv Wolfman and George Pérez, he debuted in 1980.

martian-manhunter.name: Martian Manhunter
martian-manhunter.description: J'onn J'onzz is the last survivor of Mars, teleported to Earth, where he works as a detective disguised as a human, with telepathy, shapeshifting, and flight. Created by Joseph Samachson and Joe Certa, he debuted in 1955.

joker.name: Joker
joker.description: A one-eyed, unpredictable criminal from Gotham City, Batman's greatest enemy. Created by Bill Finger, Bob Kane, and Jerry Robinson, he debuted in 1940.

catwoman.name: Catwoman
catwoman.description: Selina Kyle is a skilled Gotham City thief with a complicated relationship with Batman. Created by Bill Finger and Bob Kane, she debuted in 1940.

robin.name: Robin
robin.description: Dick Grayson is a circus acrobat who becomes Batman's partner after his parents' death. Created by Bill Finger, Bob Kane, and Jerry Robinson, he debuted in 1940.

sinestro.name: Sinestro
sinestro.description: Thaal Sinestro is a former Green Lantern who became the dictator of the planet Korugar and Green Lantern Hal Jordan's archenemy. Created by John Broome and Gil Kane, he debuted in 1961.

black-canary.name: Black Canary
black-canary.description: Dinah Lance is a martial artist and private investigator with the "Canary Cry," a sonic power capable of stunning and destroying. Created by Robert Kanigher and Carmine Infantino, she debuted in 1947.

lex-luthor.name: Lex Luthor
lex-luthor.description: A genius businessman and scientist from Metropolis, Superman's greatest enemy. Created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster, he debuted in 1940.

supergirl.name: Supergirl
supergirl.description: Kara Zor-El is Superman's cousin, also a survivor of Krypton, sharing his same superhuman powers. Created by Otto Binder and Al Plastino, she debuted in 1959.

shazam.name: Shazam
shazam.description: Billy Batson is a boy who, by saying the magic word "Shazam!", transforms into an adult hero granted powers by six mythological deities and heroes. Created by Bill Parker and C. C. Beck, he debuted in 1940.

nightwing.name: Nightwing
nightwing.description: Dick Grayson leaves the Robin identity behind and takes up the mantle of Nightwing, becoming an independent hero. Created by Marv Wolfman and George Pérez, he debuted in 1984.

deathstroke.name: Deathstroke
deathstroke.description: Slade Wilson is a mercenary and assassin enhanced by military experiments, becoming one of the Teen Titans' greatest enemies. Created by Marv Wolfman and George Pérez, he debuted in 1980.

zatanna.name: Zatanna
zatanna.description: Zatanna Zatara is a magician who casts spells by speaking words backwards, daughter of fellow magician Zatara. Created by Gardner Fox and Murphy Anderson, she debuted in 1964.

darkseid.name: Darkseid
darkseid.description: The supreme tyrant of the planet Apokolips, obsessed with discovering the Anti-Life Equation to dominate all existence. Created by Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1970.

harley-quinn.name: Harley Quinn
harley-quinn.description: Harleen Quinzel was a psychiatrist at Arkham Asylum until she fell in love with the Joker and became his partner in crime. Created by Paul Dini and Bruce Timm, she debuted in 1992.

doctor-fate.name: Doctor Fate
doctor-fate.description: Kent Nelson becomes the host of the magical helmet of the Lord of Order Nabu, gaining vast mystical powers as Doctor Fate. Created by Gardner Fox and Howard Sherman, he debuted in 1940.

black-widow.name: Black Widow
black-widow.description: Natasha Romanoff is a former spy and assassin trained by the Soviet Red Room, who becomes one of the Avengers' most skilled agents. Created by Stan Lee, Don Rico, and Don Heck, she debuted in 1964.

bucky-barnes.name: Bucky Barnes
bucky-barnes.description: James "Bucky" Barnes was Captain America's teenage partner in World War II, presumed dead and later revealed as the brainwashed assassin Winter Soldier. Created by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1941.

cyclops.name: Cyclops
cyclops.description: Scott Summers is the leader of the original X-Men, able to fire powerful force blasts from his eyes, controlled by a special visor. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1963.

elektra.name: Elektra
elektra.description: Elektra Natchios is an elite assassin trained by the Hand, and Matt Murdock's former lover. Created by Frank Miller, she debuted in 1981.

green-goblin.name: Green Goblin
green-goblin.description: Norman Osborn is an industrialist who becomes a villain after an accident with an experimental formula, and Spider-Man's greatest enemy. Created by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko, he debuted in 1964.

human-torch.name: Human Torch
human-torch.description: Johnny Storm is the youngest member of the Fantastic Four, able to burst into flame and fly without harm. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1961.

invisible-woman.name: Invisible Woman
invisible-woman.description: Sue Storm is a founding member of the Fantastic Four, with the power to turn invisible and create force fields. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, she debuted in 1961.

jean-grey.name: Jean Grey
jean-grey.description: One of the original X-Men, with telepathic and telekinetic powers later linked to the cosmic Phoenix Force. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, she debuted in 1963.

kingpin.name: Kingpin
kingpin.description: Wilson Fisk is the crime lord of New York's organized crime, a man of immense physical size and ruthless strategic mind. Created by Stan Lee and John Romita Sr., he debuted in 1967.

loki.name: Loki
loki.description: The Asgardian god of mischief, Thor's adoptive brother, driven by ambition and resentment. Created by Stan Lee, Larry Lieber, and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1962.

luke-cage.name: Luke Cage
luke-cage.description: Carl Lucas is a wrongly imprisoned man who gains unbreakable skin and superhuman strength from a prison experiment, becoming the hero for hire Luke Cage. Created by Archie Goodwin, George Tuska, and John Romita Sr., he debuted in 1972.

mary-jane.name: Mary Jane Watson
mary-jane.description: Mary Jane Watson is a New York actress and model, one of the great loves of Peter Parker's life throughout Spider-Man's history. Created by Stan Lee and John Romita Sr., her face was revealed in 1966.

mister-fantastic.name: Mister Fantastic
mister-fantastic.description: Reed Richards is the leader and chief scientist of the Fantastic Four, whose body can stretch and take any shape. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1961.

miles-morales.name: Miles Morales
miles-morales.description: A teenager who gains Spider-Man-like powers and takes up the hero's mantle in his own reality, the Ultimate Universe. Created by Brian Michael Bendis and Sara Pichelli, he debuted in 2011.

professor-x.name: Professor X
professor-x.description: Charles Xavier is a powerful telepath who founds the School for Gifted Youngsters and leads the X-Men in the fight for peaceful coexistence between humans and mutants. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1963.

she-hulk.name: She-Hulk
she-hulk.description: Jennifer Walters is a lawyer who gains her cousin Bruce Banner's powers after an emergency blood transfusion, becoming She-Hulk. Created by Stan Lee and John Buscema, she debuted in 1980.

storm.name: Storm
storm.description: Ororo Munroe is a mutant able to control the forces of nature, worshipped as a goddess in her homeland before joining the X-Men. Created by Len Wein and Dave Cockrum, she debuted in 1975.

thing.name: Thing
thing.description: Ben Grimm is a test pilot transformed into a rock-hard creature of colossal strength by the same space flight that created the Fantastic Four. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby, he debuted in 1961.

venom.name: Venom
venom.description: Eddie Brock bonds with an alien symbiote that once belonged to Spider-Man, becoming a dangerous antagonist with powers similar to the hero's. Created by David Michelinie and Todd McFarlane, he debuted in 1988.

war-machine.name: War Machine
war-machine.description: James Rhodes is Tony Stark's best friend and a military pilot who wears his own heavy combat armor. Created by David Michelinie, John Romita Sr., and Bob Layton, he debuted in 1979.
